page: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a general-purpose server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor with 48 shared vCPUs, derived from 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 2880 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and multi-core performance in the strong tier (top 25%). Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier write speeds for large blocks, while read-only large block bandwidth remains in the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI, contributing to strong scores in Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction tests. For database operations, the server demonstrates strong performance, processing 85.0 million Redis SET operations per second.

Because this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model, it offers a cost-efficient profile for high-density resource requirements, though users should consider potential resource contention tradeoffs inherent to shared environments. The combination of 192.0 GB of RAM and a 2880 GB NVMe SSD makes it suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and moderate LLM inference tasks. Text generation for small and medium models achieves top-tier speeds, while larger model processing remains in the average tier.
description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 48 shared vCPUs and 24 physical cores. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2880 GB NVMe SSD. Computational benchmarks place its single-core performance in the top tier and multi-core performance in the strong tier. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, processing 85.0 million Redis SET operations per second, and exhibits top-tier large-block memory write bandwidth. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs, the high memory density and local NVMe storage make this server cost-efficient for database hosting, static web serving, and moderate language model inference workloads.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a general-purpose server with 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and a 2880 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong database and multi-core benchmark performance.

tagline: A high-density general-purpose server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors, substantial memory, and fast local NVMe storage for database workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory paired with a 2880 GB NVMe SSD
